frontier: Can a GNFS route be reopened only after an auditable sealed-runner nonemission gate, rather than by repeating an unchanged checkpoint?
action: Designed and statically verified a six-obligation sealed no-factorer/nonemission contract; no runner or measurement was invoked.
verified: Replication passed all seven checks: contract completeness, defer-by-default admission, design-only status, unchanged queue, no runtime proof attempt, matching classification, and forbidden-side-effect absence.
result: A bounded next-experiment design is ready. It makes no GNFS-performance, factorization, or runtime-nonemission claim; execution remains deferred.
next_gate: Require explicit authorization or a concrete sealed-runner, manifest, planned-row, fixture, lineage, prior-receipt, or protected-hash change; then prove runner nonemission before any measurement.
boundary_held: No production Atlas writes, external submissions, git pushes, or training unless explicitly authorized.
